title: HTML Lists
content:
### HTML Lists HTML supports **three** types of lists. --- ### List Types | Type | Tag | Purpose | |------|-----|---------| | **Unordered** | `
    ` | Bullet points | | **Ordered** | `
      ` | Numbered items | | **Description** | `
      ` | Terms and definitions | --- ### Unordered List ```html
      • Apple
      • Banana
      • Cherry
      ``` ### Ordered List ```html
      1. First step
      2. Second step
      3. Third step
      ``` ### Description List ```html
      HTML
      HyperText Markup Language
      CSS
      Cascading Style Sheets
      ``` --- ### Nested Lists Lists can be placed inside other list items: ```html
      • Fruits
        • Apple
        • Banana
      • Vegetables
        • Carrot
      ``` --- > **Best Practice:** Use `
        ` when order does not matter, `
          ` when order matters (steps, rankings), and `
          ` for glossaries or key-value pairs.
codeExample:
Shopping List (Unordered)

  • Milk
  • Bread
  • Eggs

Recipe Steps (Ordered)

  1. Preheat oven
  2. Mix ingredients
  3. Bake for 30 min

Glossary (Description)

HTML
Markup language for web pages

quiz.question: Which tag creates an unordered list?
